title: 10 best herbal teas for high blood pressure
intro_desc: Herbal teas for high blood pressure are caffeine-free beverages made from various plants, flowers, and seeds, which have been used in traditional medicine to help manage hypertension. These teas often contain compounds that promote vasodilation, improve circulation, and reduce inflammation, all of which contribute to lowering blood pressure. Examples of herbal teas beneficial for high blood pressure include hibiscus, green tea, chamomile, and Hawthorn. By incorporating these teas into a daily routine, individuals can potentially reduce their reliance on medication, lower their risk of heart disease, and enhance their overall quality of life. For instance, a study published in the Journal of Hypertension found that consuming hibiscus tea for six weeks significantly reduced systolic and diastolic blood pressure in mildly hypertensive adults.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Common hawthorn teas helps with high blood pressure because it contains flavonoids, anthocyanins, and procyanidins that work together to relax and dilate blood vessels, reducing peripheral resistance and lowering blood pressure. The tea also improves blood vessel function, allowing blood to flow more freely and efficiently, which can help reduce the workload on the heart and lower blood pressure. Additionally, hawthorn tea may help to strengthen the heart's pumping action, further contributing to its potential benefits in managing hypertension.
remedy_properties: Vasodilatory effects: Hawthorn's flavonoids, such as quercetin and epicatechin, help to dilate blood vessels, reducing blood pressure by allowing for smoother blood flow and lowering peripheral resistance.

Cardiac glycoside content: Hawthorn contains cardiac glycosides like digitoxin, which can help regulate heart rhythm and strengthen the heart's contractions, improving circulation and reducing symptoms of hypertension.

Antioxidant properties: Hawthorn's rich antioxidant content helps protect the cardiovascular system from oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to high blood pressure by damaging arterial walls and promoting atherosclerosis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Hawthorn roots have been traditionally used to help lower blood pressure by relaxing blood vessels and improving circulation.
Leaves: The leaves of the hawthorn plant contain flavonoids, which have been shown to improve blood vessel function and help manage high blood pressure.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried common hawthorn flowers and berries.
Boil 8 ounces of water in a tea kettle for 4-6 minutes.
Add the dried herb mixture to the boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup.
Allow the tea to cool for 2-3 minutes before drinking immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Midland hawthorn teas helps with high blood pressure because it contains flavonoids, which help to relax blood vessels and improve blood flow. This relaxation of the blood vessels reduces blood pressure and also improves overall cardiovascular health. Additionally, Midland hawthorn tea has been shown to reduce inflammation in the body, which is a common underlying cause of high blood pressure. By reducing inflammation and improving blood vessel function, Midland hawthorn tea can help to lower blood pressure and promote overall heart health.
remedy_properties: Vasodilation: Midland Hawthorn tea's flavonoids, particularly quercetin and rutin, help dilate blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing blood pressure by relaxing the smooth muscle in vessel walls.

Anti-inflammatory: The antioxidants and polyphenols present in Midland Hawthorn tea have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the blood vessels and improve blood vessel function, thereby lowering blood pressure.

Cardioprotective: The bioactive compounds in Midland Hawthorn tea, such as oligomeric proanthocyanidins (OPCs), have been shown to protect against cardiovascular disease by improving blood lipid profiles, reducing blood viscosity, and preventing platelet aggregation, all of which contribute to lowering high blood pressure.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Midland Hawthorn plant are rich in flavonoids and saponins, which have been traditionally used to help lower blood pressure by improving cardiovascular health.
Bark: The bark of the Midland Hawthorn plant contains a significant amount of quercetin, a flavonoid that has been shown to help relax blood vessels and reduce blood pressure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried midland hawthorn flowers, leaves, and berries in the ratio of 2:1:1.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm dark place for 24 hours to reduce moisture content.
Combine one teaspoon of dried midland hawthorn mixture with boiling water in a teapot.
Steep the tea mixture for 10-15 minutes, then strain and discard solids before serving.
Consume the tea hot or cold within three days of preparation and store any leftover in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender teas helps with high blood pressure because it contains compounds that help to relax and dilate blood vessels, reducing tension and improving circulation. The calming properties of lavender also have a positive impact on the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety which can contribute to hypertension. Additionally, lavender tea has been shown to lower blood pressure by inhibiting the production of hormones that constrict blood vessels, ultimately leading to a decrease in blood pressure readings.
remedy_properties: Vasodilatory effects: The flavonoids present in lavender tea can help relax blood vessels, reducing blood pressure by allowing them to expand and increase blood flow.
Antioxidant activity: Lavender tea's antioxidant compounds can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which are both thought to contribute to high blood pressure.
Anxiolytic properties: The calming effects of lavender tea may help reduce anxiety and stress levels, which are common triggers for hypertension.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the English lavender plant contain flavonoids, which have been shown to help relax blood vessels and lower blood pressure.
Seeds: Lavender seeds are rich in essential oils that can help calm the nervous system and reduce stress, both of which are beneficial for individuals with high blood pressure.
remedy_recipe: Measure one tablespoon of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers for every eight ounces water.
Heat fresh or filtered water in a pot to a simmering temperature of around 195 to 205 degrees fahrenheit.
Pour the hot water over the measured dried lavender flowers and let it steep for five to seven minutes.
Strain the tea mixture using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the lavender solids.
Serve the tea hot, sweeten with honey if desired, and enjoy the calming aroma and flavor within 20-30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Danshen teas helps with high blood pressure because they contain a unique compound called salvia miltiorrhiza, which has been shown to have a calming effect on blood vessels. This reduces resistance in the arteries and allows blood to flow more smoothly, thus lowering blood pressure. Additionally, danshen teas can help to reduce inflammation in the body, another factor that contributes to high blood pressure. By drinking danshen tea regularly, individuals may find that their blood pressure levels become more stable and manageable.
remedy_properties: Salvianolic acid B: This compound has been shown to reduce blood pressure by relaxing blood vessels, improving blood flow, and inhibiting the production of vasopressor hormones that constrict blood vessels.
Danshensu: Danshensu is a potent antioxidant that helps to neutralize free radicals in the body, which can contribute to oxidative stress and blood vessel damage, leading to high blood pressure.

Cryptotanshinone: This compound has been found to inhibit the activity of enzymes involved in angiotensin-converting enzyme (ACE), which is responsible for converting angiotensin I into angiotensin II, a potent vasoconstrictor that can increase blood pressure.
remedy_parts: Roots: Danshen roots are rich in tanshinone, a compound that has been shown to help lower blood pressure by improving cardiovascular function and reducing inflammation.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzome of the danshen plant contains saponins, which have been found to exhibit hypotensive effects, helping to relax blood vessels and reduce blood pressure.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried danshen root salvia miltiorrhiza into a tea infuser or strainer.
Boil one cup of fresh water in a saucepan and pour it over the danshen root.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, releasing the active compounds into the water.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a teapot or mug, discarding solids.
Let the tea cool slightly before serving and consume within 10-15 minutes of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary teas helps with high blood pressure because it is rich in antioxidants, which help to reduce inflammation and improve circulation. The tea's active compounds, such as carnosic acid and rosmarinic acid, have been shown to relax blood vessels and increase blood flow, thereby reducing blood pressure. Additionally, rosemary's anti-inflammatory properties may also help to reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease, a common complication of high blood pressure.
remedy_properties: Vasodilatory compounds: Rosemary tea contains vasodilatory compounds like camphor and borneol, which can help relax blood vessels and improve blood flow, reducing blood pressure.
Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in rosemary tea, such as rosmarinic acid, may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress that can contribute to high blood pressure.

Calcium-channel blocker effects: Rosemary's flavonoids have been shown to exert calcium-channel blocking properties, which can help lower blood pressure by reducing the force of muscle contractions in blood vessels.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and essential oils, rosemary leaves help to dilate blood vessels and improve circulation, reducing blood pressure.
Roots: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, rosemary roots have been traditionally used to lower blood pressure by improving cardiovascular health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried rosemary leaves, using scissors or mortar and pestle, measuring out 2 teaspoons for every 8 ounces water.
Combine the rosemary leaves with boiling water in a teapot or infuser, steeping for 5-7 minutes to allow flavors to meld.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a separate container, discarding solids and retaining flavorful liquid.
Chill the tea in refrigerator for at least 2 hours before serving over ice cubes with lemon slices if desired.
Serve rosemary tea hot or cold, adjusting sweetness to taste with honey or sugar, enjoying within 24 hours of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: European mistletoe teas helps with high blood pressure because it contains compounds like viscopinin, which has been shown to relax blood vessels and improve blood flow. The tea's flavonoids also help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, both of which can contribute to hypertension. Additionally, mistletoe extracts have been found to lower blood pressure by reducing the activity of enzymes that constrict blood vessels, making it a natural and potentially effective way to manage high blood pressure without medication.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic effects: European mistletoe tea has been shown to help regulate blood pressure by adapting to the body's changing needs, reducing stress-induced hypertension and promoting overall cardiovascular health. This helps reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease associated with high blood pressure.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in European mistletoe tea help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation, which are common underlying factors contributing to high blood pressure, thus reducing the damage caused by free radicals.

Cardiotonic properties: Mistletoe tea has been traditionally used as a cardiotonic agent, helping to strengthen and stabilize the heart muscles, improve circulation, and reduce blood pressure by increasing peripheral resistance.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and alkaloids, roots help to dilate blood vessels and reduce blood pressure.
Bark: Containing tannins and other compounds, bark helps to strengthen blood vessel walls and improve cardiovascular health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried viscum album leaves and stems from reputable sources.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried mistletoe herbs for every 8 ounces of boiling water.
Heat the mixture in a saucepan for 5-10 minutes or until the water reaches a rolling boil.
Reduce heat to low simmer for an additional 10-15 minutes and let the flavors infuse.
Strain the tea into cups and discard the solids, then enjoy as is or with sweeteners.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Garlic teas helps with high blood pressure because of its potent combination of bioactive compounds, including allicin, which has natural vasodilatory properties. Drinking herbal garlic tea regularly can help to relax and dilate blood vessels, allowing for easier blood flow and reduced resistance in the circulatory system, resulting in lower blood pressure levels. This natural remedy also contains antioxidants that help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, further contributing to its beneficial effects on cardiovascular health.
remedy_properties: Allicin: A sulfur compound found in garlic, allicin has been shown to help lower blood pressure by relaxing blood vessels and reducing inflammation, which can contribute to hypertension.

Quercetin: A flavonoid antioxidant present in garlic, quercetin helps to reduce blood vessel constriction, improve blood flow, and lower blood pressure by inhibiting the action of enzymes that constrict blood vessels.

S-Allyl cysteine (SAC): A sulfur compound found in garlic tea, SAC has been shown to help lower blood pressure by inhibiting the production of angiotensin-converting enzyme (ACE), a key player in the renin-angiotensin-aldosterone system that regulates blood pressure.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and bioactive compounds, garlic roots help to lower blood pressure by improving blood flow and reducing inflammation.
Rhyzomes: Containing a higher concentration of allicin than other parts, garlic rhyzomes are effective in relaxing blood vessels and decreasing blood pressure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh garlic bulbs from your garden or purchase organic garlic cloves.
Peel the garlic cloves and chop them into small pieces weighing approximately 3-4 grams.
Combine the chopped garlic with one liter of boiling water in a large pot.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes to allow the flavors to infuse and blend.
Strain the liquid using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cinnamon teas helps with high blood pressure because of its unique ability to improve circulation and reduce inflammation. The active compound cinnamaldehyde in cinnamon has been shown to help relax blood vessels, allowing for smoother blood flow and lower blood pressure readings. Additionally, the tea's antioxidants work to counteract oxidative stress, which can contribute to hypertension. By addressing these underlying factors, herbal cinnamon teas can provide a natural and effective way to manage high blood pressure.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Cinnamon's antioxidants help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to hypertension.
Anti-inflammatory activity: Cinnamon's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as cinnamic acid, can help lower blood pressure by reducing inflammation in blood vessels and improving vasodilation.
Antithrombotic activity: Cinnamon's ability to inhibit platelet aggregation and blood clotting can help reduce the risk of cardiovascular events associated with high blood pressure.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in compounds like cinnamic acid, which has been shown to have a positive effect on blood pressure by relaxing blood vessels and improving circulation.
Bark: Contains coumarin, a natural anticoagulant that helps to reduce blood clotting and improve blood flow, making it effective for high blood pressure management.
remedy_recipe: Grind one teaspoon of dried cinnamomum verum bark into fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Add one cup of boiling water to the powdered cinnamon in a tea infuser or heat-resistant cup.
Allow the mixture to steep for five to seven minutes, then strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Add honey or lemon to taste, stirring well to combine before serving immediately.
Discard any remaining solids and store the cooled tea in an airtight container for up to three days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Large-leaved lime teas helps with high blood pressure because its unique blend of antioxidants, flavonoids, and other compounds work together to relax and dilate blood vessels, reducing resistance and improving blood flow. This natural vasodilation helps to lower blood pressure levels, easing the strain on the cardiovascular system and promoting overall heart health. Additionally, the tea's anti-inflammatory properties may also help reduce inflammation in the body, which is a known contributor to high blood pressure.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Large-leaved lime tea contains a rich source of antioxidants, which help to combat oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, reducing the risk of cardiovascular disease and high blood pressure.

Potassium-rich: Large-leaved lime tea is a natural source of potassium, an essential mineral that helps to lower blood pressure by counteracting the effects of sodium and promoting healthy blood vessel function.

Calcium-channel blockers: The flavonoids present in large-leaved lime tea have been shown to exhibit calcium-channel blocking activity, which can help to relax blood vessels and reduce blood pressure.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and saponins, the roots are believed to help lower blood pressure by reducing inflammation and improving blood vessel function.
Bark: Containing compounds like berberine and hesperidin, the bark has been shown to relax blood vessels and improve circulation, making it a natural remedy for hypertension.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh tilia platyphyllos leaves, selecting only those with no signs of disease or pests for optimal flavor and quality.
Rinse the harvested leaves thoroughly with cold filtered water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine 20-25 grams of dried or 40-50 grams of fresh tilia platyphyllos leaves with one liter of boiling water in a large glass teapot.
Steep the herbal mixture for 5-7 minutes, allowing the flavors and active compounds to meld and blend together harmoniously.
Strain the tea into cups using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, discarding the solids and serving hot or chilled as desired.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Littleleaf linden teas helps with high blood pressure because of its unique combination of flavonoids, minerals, and antioxidants. The tea has been shown to help relax blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing peripheral resistance, which can lead to a decrease in blood pressure. Additionally, the tea's anti-inflammatory properties may also play a role in reducing inflammation in the body, another common contributor to high blood pressure.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant compounds: Littleleaf linden tea contains antioxidant compounds that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to high blood pressure by damaging blood vessels and increasing vascular resistance.

Calcium channel blockers: The flavonoids present in littleleaf linden tea have been shown to act as calcium channel blockers, which helps to relax blood vessels and lower blood pressure by reducing the amount of calcium ions entering blood vessel walls.

Cardiac glycosides: Littleleaf linden tea contains cardiac glycosides that help regulate heart rate and contractility, thereby reducing blood pressure by slowing down the heartbeat and decreasing the force of each beat.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and saponins, roots of littleleaf linden plant help reduce blood pressure by relaxing blood vessels and improving circulation.
Bark: The bark of littleleaf linden contains salicylic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried tilia cordata leaves for a minimum of one ounce per serving.
Rinse the harvested leaves with filtered water to remove any dirt or debris gently.
Steep one teaspoon of crushed or whole tilia cordata leaves in boiling water for seven minutes carefully.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup and discard solids quickly.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before serving and consuming immediately or storing refrigerated for up to 24 hours safely.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat high blood pressure with herbal teas is by consuming at least two cups daily of tea made from hibiscus flowers, which have been shown to lower blood pressure. Another option is green tea, which contains catechins that can help dilate blood vessels and reduce blood pressure. Additionally, teas made from herbs like mistletoe, yarrow, and holly have been used in traditional medicine to treat hypertension. However, it's important to consult with a healthcare professional before starting any new treatment regimen.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to high blood pressure that are treated with herbal teas include mild anxiety, stress, and insomnia. For instance, chamomile tea is known for its calming properties and can help reduce stress and promote sleep. Similarly, passionflower tea has a relaxing effect on the body and is often used to treat anxiety and insomnia. Additionally, hawthorn tea is known for its ability to dilate blood vessels, which can help reduce blood pressure and improve circulation. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare professional before using herbal teas as a treatment for any medical condition.
